name: Mabon
description: Mabon ap Modron, meaning "Great Son of the Divine Mother," is a prominent figure in Welsh mythology. His story revolves around his abduction as an infant and his eventual rescue, a theme common in Celtic lore. Modron, his mother, is associated with the Matronae, Celtic mother goddesses. Mabon's imprisonment is often linked to the Otherworld, and his liberation is crucial for the hero Culhwch in the tale of Culhwch and Olwen. To free Olwen, Culhwch must complete a series of impossible tasks, and Mabon's aid is essential for the boar hunt of Twrch Trwyth. Mabon's knowledge of hunting and the land, gained during his confinement, proves invaluable. He represents youth, fertility, and the cyclical nature of life, death, and rebirth. His connection to the land and his eventual return symbolize the restoration of balance and prosperity. He is a skilled hunter and warrior, embodying the ideal of the young hero. Mabon's story highlights themes of perseverance, resilience, and the importance of familial bonds. He is a powerful symbol of hope and the potential for renewal even in the darkest of times. His role in the hunt of Twrch Trwyth solidifies his status as a vital ally and a key player in Welsh mythological narratives. He is a testament to the strength of the human spirit and the enduring power of myth.
subtitle: The Great Son of the Divine Mother
achievements: Participated in the hunt of Twrch Trwyth
Provided crucial knowledge for the hunt

keyMyths.title: The Abduction of Mabon
keyMyths.description: Mabon is stolen from his mother, Modron, when he is only three nights old. His whereabouts remain unknown for many years, causing distress and disrupting the natural order.

keyMyths.title: Mabon's Imprisonment
keyMyths.description: Mabon is imprisoned in Caer Loyw (Gloucester) or Caer Galch (possibly Chalk). The exact reason for his imprisonment is unclear, but it is believed to be related to his divine lineage.

keyMyths.title: The Hunt of Twrch Trwyth
keyMyths.description: Mabon's assistance is crucial in the hunt for the monstrous boar, Twrch Trwyth. His knowledge of the land and hunting skills are essential to capturing the boar's treasures.

sources.author: Lady Charlotte Guest
sources.description: A collection of Welsh tales including Culhwch and Olwen, which features Mabon.
